notes: Substrate-class tasks: deterministic bytes-in/bytes-out compute, language-neutral. Vectors are the full acceptance contract; prompts state exactly what solutions receive and must emit.

tasks.title: Uppercase ASCII
tasks.prompt: Read all input bytes. Output the same bytes with ASCII lowercase letters a-z converted to uppercase. All other bytes pass through unchanged.

tasks.title: Count newline bytes
tasks.prompt: Read all input bytes. Output the number of 0x0A (newline) bytes as decimal ASCII digits with no trailing newline.

tasks.title: Sum integer lines
tasks.prompt: Input is zero or more lines, each a non-negative decimal integer (< 1000000), separated by 0x0A newlines; a trailing newline may be absent and empty lines must be ignored. Output the sum as decimal ASCII with no trailing newline.

tasks.title: Most frequent word
tasks.prompt: Tokenize input into words: maximal runs of ASCII letters a-z/A-Z, lowercased; every other byte separates words. Output 'word count' (single 0x20 between word and decimal count, no trailing newline) for the most frequent word; break count ties by lexicographically smallest word. Input contains at least one word.

vectors.input: The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog the fox

tasks.title: Run-length encode
tasks.prompt: Read all input bytes. For each maximal run of identical bytes, output the byte itself followed by the run length as decimal ASCII digits. Output nothing for empty input.

tasks.title: Extract JSON field
tasks.prompt: Input is a single JSON object whose 'name' member is a JSON string containing no escape sequences. Output the raw string value of 'name' (no quotes, no trailing newline). The object may contain other members in any order; whitespace may vary.
